primary_diagnosis.description: Malignant neoplasm of upper lobe, unspecified bronchus or lung

policy_excerpts.excerpt_text: Pembrolizumab monotherapy is medically necessary for first-line metastatic NSCLC when ALL: (1) Stage IV NSCLC, (2) PD-L1 expression ≥ 1% by IHC 22C3 (or pending if specimen indeterminate, see § Specimen Inadequacy), (3) EGFR wild-type AND ALK-negative, (4) ECOG 0-1.
When PD-L1 specimen is indeterminate, repeat biopsy is preferred. If repeat is not feasible, NCCN-aligned exception may be considered.

criteria.evidence: PD-L1 indeterminate; specimen insufficient (page 3 of pathology report)
Both confirmed negative on submitted molecular panel

necessity_assessment.summary: Three of four criteria met. PD-L1 expression criterion fails because specimen was insufficient — see § Specimen Inadequacy for the exception path.

decision.rationale: Pembrolizumab is denied because the PD-L1 expression criterion (AETNA-MED-2024-014 § First-line metastatic NSCLC monotherapy, criterion 2) is not met — the submitted PD-L1 IHC 22C3 specimen was insufficient and returned indeterminate. The other three criteria (Stage IV, EGFR/ALK status, ECOG) are met. Per § Specimen Inadequacy, repeat biopsy is preferred; if not feasible, an NCCN-aligned exception may be considered via the appeal path.

denial_forecast.reasoning: The denial is on a procedural criterion (specimen) rather than a clinical contraindication. Repeat biopsy or NCCN-aligned exception under § Specimen Inadequacy has a high overturn rate per Aetna 2025 internal-appeal data.

appeal_draft.appeal_body:
RE: Appeal of Pembrolizumab Denial — Patient MR — Authrex case DEMO-002

Dear Aetna Medical Reviewer,

This appeal is filed under Aetna Medical Policy AETNA-MED-2024-014 § Specimen Inadequacy (page 6). The initial denial cited insufficient PD-L1 IHC 22C3 specimen — an indeterminate result, not a confirmed PD-L1 < 1% expression. The other three criteria for first-line pembrolizumab monotherapy are met: (1) Stage IV NSCLC confirmed, (3) EGFR wild-type and ALK-negative, (4) ECOG 1.

NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology — Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer v.3.2025 § NSCL-G recommends pembrolizumab monotherapy for first-line metastatic NSCLC when PD-L1 IHC is unavailable AND EGFR/ALK are negative — the exact clinical scenario for this patient.

We respectfully request reconsideration under the NCCN-aligned exception path explicitly permitted by § Specimen Inadequacy. The patient's stage IV status, performance status, and molecular profile all support pembrolizumab as the first-line standard-of-care treatment.

Attached: Pathology report (showing specimen insufficiency), molecular profile, NCCN NSCL-G guideline excerpt, ECOG status notation.

Respectfully submitted,
[Provider name and credentials]

structured_arguments.argument: PD-L1 status is indeterminate, not negative
Three of four criteria are MET
NCCN NSCL-G recommends pembrolizumab monotherapy in this scenario
structured_arguments.evidence_citation: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology — NSCLC v.3.2025

patient_communication.summary_text: Your insurance has not yet approved pembrolizumab. The reason is a lab test (PD-L1) that didn't have enough tissue to read. Your doctor is filing an appeal that explains why pembrolizumab is still the right treatment based on your other test results and national guidelines. Most appeals like this are approved within 14 days.
next_action_steps: Wait for the appeal decision (typically 14 days)
Your doctor may also request a repeat biopsy as a backup path
Call your care coordinator if you have questions about treatment timing
